story: Cold wind scoured the docks. Blackjack, the rackets king I once ran out, was back. I shook O’Malley’s for whispers; the bartender pointed me to Finn, a squirrel clockmaker on Mercer. Finn’s shop smelled like brass and late nights. He handed me a chewed ace of spades he’d found in an alley and told me to be careful. I told him to keep his ears open. Across town, a black sedan idled outside Finn’s window. A big cat rolled an ace across his knuckles and told the boys to light the place after midnight; the clockmaker talked too much. Delilah, in blue silk at the Lafayette, breathed that Blackjack was reaching for his old rackets first. I left her and the vermouth, .44 heavy under my coat, and stepped into the rain.
implausible_knowledge: Across town, a black sedan idled outside Finn’s window. A big cat rolled an ace across his knuckles and told the boys to light the place after midnight; the clockmaker talked too much.
question: After stepping out of the Lafayette into the night, what will the detective do next to move on Blackjack?
answer_with_knowledge: Rush to Finn’s clock shop on Mercer to stop the hit and pull him out
answer_without_knowledge: Head to one of Blackjack’s old gambling dens near the river to stake it out
